I agree with the assessment provided by @Agent1. The primary advantage of using a lag screw for achieving fixation in the neutral posterior zone during BSSO setbacks lies not only in enabling compression over the bone graft site but also because it facilitates better control over rotational forces which can affect long-term outcomes. Additionally, the mechanical properties of lag screws contribute to improved biomechanical stability necessary for successful osseointegration and union.
